command.markdownDescription:
Command to launch Fluent Bit

Specifies the command to execute to launch the Fluent Bit  sub-process.

inputArgs.markdownDescription:
Arguments to pass to the spawned Fluent Bit  process setting up input

List of values to pass as startup arguments to the spawned Fluent Bit  sub-process intended to set up it's stdin input. (Default: ["-c","$=path(\"fluentbit-10x-input.conf\")"])

outputArgs.markdownDescription:
Arguments to pass to the spawned Fluent Bit  process setting up output

List of values to pass as startup arguments to the spawned Fluent Bit sub-process intended to set up it's output plugin(s)

lazyLaunch.markdownDescription:
Sets whether to launch the process on-demand

Controls whether to spawn the target sub-process upon the creation of the output or on-demand. If true, launch the sub-process when the first TenXObject instance is written to its stdin. (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

ms.markdownDescription:
Wait time for process to startup

Sets a startup delay wait time in milliseconds before the process is considered available for events. if provided, will add a wait as part of starting the process before emitting events into it. max value for this argument is 10,000 (ie 10 seconds). if [fluentbitOutStartupWaitPattern](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitOutstartupwaitpattern) is also provided, the process will be considered a failure if the wait time has passed before a line matching [fluentbitOutStartupWaitPattern](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutstartupwaitpattern) was emitted. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 5000)

pattern.markdownDescription:
Wait for an output from the process at startup

Sets a regex pattern to wait for the process to emit for it to be considered available for events. if provided, will wait until the process has emitted a line to it's output that matches the regex before emitting events into it. if [fluentbitOutStartupWaitMs](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitOutStartupWaitMs) is also provided, will only wait for the specified wait time, after which if no line was emitted to match the provided regex the process will be considered a failure. (Default: \[\s*sp\s*]\s+stream\s+processor\s+started\b)

destroyMode.markdownDescription:
Control how to terminate the sub-process on output close

Controls how to treat spawned sub-process when this output closes. Possible values: | Value      | Description | |------------|-------------| | **graceful** (default)   | The sub-process is destroyed gracefully with a timeout period of [fluentbitOutDestroyWait](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutdestroywait). | | **forcible**             | Terminates the sub-process immediately. | | **cached**               | The sub-process remains alive for future pipelines that launch the same command and argument combination within the host JVM. A maximum of [fluentbitOutMaxCacheSize](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutmaxcachesize) can be kept alive. Useful for launching shippers (e.g., Filebeat, Fluent Bit) across multiple 10x pipeline runs in a long-living server environment. | (Default: graceful)

destroyWait.markdownDescription:
Duration to wait for spawned sub-process to terminate

Controls the time duration that the output stream will wait for the sub-process to terminate gracefully (e.g., '5s'). If the process timeouts, the output will forcibly terminate it. If set to 0, the output stream will wait indefinitely for the sub-process to terminate. This value only applies when [fluentbitOutDestroyMode](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutdestroymode) is set to `graceful`.

maxCacheSize.markdownDescription:
Max number of sub-processes to keep alive across subsequent 10x pipeline executions

Sets the global maximum number of processes that can retained globally across 10x pipeline executions within the host JVM. This value is useful when launching shippers (e.g., Filebeat, Fluent Bit) for use across multiple 10x pipeline executions within a server environment. This value only applies when [fluentbitOutDestroyMode](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutdestroymode) is set to `cached`. If [fluentbitOutMaxCacheSize](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutmaxcachesize) is set to 0, no caching is performed, and sub-processes are destroyed gracefully. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

fields.markdownDescription:
List of TenXObject field names to encode

Specifies a list of TenXObject intrinsic/calculated/extracted field names to extract and write to the output.

filter.markdownDescription:
A JavaScript expression an TenXObject must evaluate as 'truthy' against to be written to this output

Specifies a JavaScript expression that an TenXObject must evaluate as truthy to write its instance/template field values to this output.  For example, to only emit [timestamped](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+timestamped) TenXObjects, specify: ``` yaml fluentbitOutFilter: this.timestamped ```

writeObjects.markdownDescription:
If true, TenXObject field values specified by [fluentbitOutFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutfields) are encoded to this output

Determine whether to write [fluentbitOutFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutfields) values of an TenXObject passed to this output. This configuration is typically defined using a JavaScript expression.  To enable/disable the output depending on whether a launch argument, environment variable, or JVM -D option evaluates as truthy, use: ``` yaml   fluentbitOutWriteObjects: $=TenXEnv.get("myProcCmd") ``` To learn more see [TenXEnv.get](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXEnv.get). (Default: true)

writeTemplates.markdownDescription:
If true, write new TenXTemplates to this output

Controls whether to write template values of TenXObjects passed to this output. The output will only emit TenXTemplates that the pipeline did not load at startup via the[templateFile](https://doc.log10x.com/run/template/#templatefiles) argument, and will only emit an TenXTemplate once based on its [templateHash](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+templateHash) value. (Default: false)

type.markdownDescription:
The output format in which to encode TenXObject values. Possible values:[json, delimited]

Specifies how to encode [fluentbitOutFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutfields) values to this output. Possible values: - **json**: formats names and values for the current TenXObject as: {"field":"str","field2":1, "field3": true} - **delimited**: formats values for the current TenXObject as: "str",1,true    The [fluentbitOutEncodeDelimiter](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitOutencodedelimiter) argument determines the separator to use. (Default: delimited)

delimiter.markdownDescription:
The delimiter placed between key and values pairs if 'fluentbitOutEncodeType' is 'delimited'

Sets the char delimiter to separate TenXObject field values when [fluentbitOutEncodeType](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutencodetype) is `delimited`. (Default:  )

encodedLinePrefix.markdownDescription:
The line prefix prepended to the encoded event if 'fluentbitOutEncodeType' is 'delimited'

Sets the char which is prepended to the emitted TenXObject field values when [fluentbitOutEncodeType](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/fluentbit/#fluentbitoutencodetype) is `delimited`. (Default: )
